gene_description: Synaptosomal-Associated Protein 25 — essential component of the SNARE complex mediating neurotransmitter vesicle fusion and release. Modulates dopaminergic and noradrenergic transmission relevant to ADHD and stimulant response.

source.title: Pharmacogenetics of methylphenidate in childhood attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder: long-term effects
source.finding: SNAP25 rs3746544 G allele carriers showed poorer methylphenidate response — only 33.3% of G/G genotype responded well vs 74.7% of T/T, with 2-3x higher risk of irritability and sleep problems during treatment.

GG.effect: Homozygous for the G allele (MnlI polymorphism 1065T>G). Only 33.3% of individuals with G/G genotype showed good clinical response to methylphenidate. G allele carriers were 2-3 times more likely to experience irritability and sleep problems during treatment. The variant affects SNAP25 expression and synaptic vesicle dynamics, impairing neurotransmitter release efficiency.
GG.recommendation: If methylphenidate is initiated, monitor closely for treatment-resistant symptoms and side effects (especially irritability and sleep disturbance). Consider amphetamine-based alternatives, atomoxetine, or guanfacine if response is inadequate. Behavioral interventions may have greater relative benefit.

GT.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Approximately 72.5% of T/G carriers showed good response to methylphenidate, significantly better than G/G but slightly less than T/T genotype. Moderate increase in risk of treatment-related irritability.
GT.recommendation: Standard methylphenidate dosing is reasonable. Monitor for irritability and sleep problems. Adjust formulation timing if sleep disturbance occurs.

TG.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Approximately 72.5% of T/G carriers showed good response to methylphenidate, significantly better than G/G but slightly less than T/T genotype. Moderate increase in risk of treatment-related irritability.
TG.recommendation: Standard methylphenidate dosing is reasonable. Monitor for irritability and sleep problems. Adjust formulation timing if sleep disturbance occurs.

TT.effect: Homozygous for the reference T allele. 74.7% of individuals with this genotype showed good clinical response to methylphenidate. Normal SNAP25 expression and synaptic vesicle function. Lower risk of treatment-emergent irritability and sleep problems.
TT.recommendation: Standard methylphenidate dosing per ADHD guidelines. Good likelihood of treatment response.
